---
title: "US Dataset Preparation Code"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

## US Dataset Preparation Code

- This code is being written in R to merge all 10 individual cities and prepare the final merge data set for whole US market. 
- At max from each individuall city, only 2500 records are being selected.
- For cities like Chicago, LA, SFO as the data set contains more than 2500 records, so randomly 2500 records being selected
- For rest cities, all the data is being considered. 

```{r}
#Setting the directory to read the data set
setwd("/Users/pooja/Desktop/GT_College/08_MGT_6203/Group_Project/Data_Wrangling/final_filter_data_set")
getwd()


#Reading data sets for all trained 10 cities.
boston_dataset <- read.csv("boston_merge.csv")
columbus_dataset <- read.csv("columbus_merge.csv")
dallas_dataset <- read.csv("dallas_merge.csv")
denver_dataset <- read.csv("denver_merge.csv")
portland_dataset <- read.csv("portland_merge.csv")
seattle_dataset <- read.csv("seattle_merge.csv")
chicago_dataset <- read.csv("chicago_merge.csv")
sfo_dataset <- read.csv("sfo_merge.csv")
la_dataset <- read.csv("la_merge.csv")
newyork_dataset <- read.csv("newyork_merge.csv")

#Randomly selecting only 2500 records for the data sets having more than
# 2500 records
chicago_2500 <- chicago_dataset[sample(nrow(chicago_dataset), 2500), ]
sfo_2500 <- sfo_dataset[sample(nrow(sfo_dataset), 2500), ]
la_2500 <- la_dataset[sample(nrow(la_dataset), 2500), ]
newyork_2500 <- newyork_dataset[sample(nrow(newyork_dataset), 2500), ]

#merging all 10 city data
merged_dataset <- rbind(boston_dataset, columbus_dataset, dallas_dataset,
                        denver_dataset, portland_dataset, seattle_dataset,
                        chicago_2500, sfo_2500, la_2500, newyork_2500)

#Validating the merged data set
head(merged_dataset, 5)

#Checking the total count of the merged data set
nrow(merged_dataset)

#Writing data set to a csv file
write.csv(merged_dataset,"merge_dataset.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
